title: Neon–Perturbation–Shard: Importance Assessment
decision_horizon: Before any public MCORE-1, AI-interpretability, or distributed-systems claim derived from this source
receipt_manifest: External source retained outside this public repository; see the paired Markdown report Trace section.
next_action: Implement PSA-001 as the first falsifiable perturbation × shard experiment.
signal: The source is a high-leverage research-intake artifact because it surfaces a testable MCORE-1 question about controlled perturbations, shard boundaries, and explicit boundary-state handoff. It is not evidence for an MCORE-1, AI-safety, or neon-physics claim.

proof.statement: The source describes perturbation theory as a framework for controlled deviations and sharding as a pattern for partitioned computation.
proof.receipt: Neon • Perturbation Theory • Database Sharding — Meta-Analysis Report, June 2026, executive summary and Sections 3–4.

proof.statement: The strongest transferable idea is the Perturbation × Shard dyad: assess local perturbations, boundary-state transfer, and recomposition behavior in a deterministic state system.
The source acknowledges sparse direct cross-literature and proposes sharded perturbation simulation as future work.

known_limits: The source does not provide a reproducible corpus method, screening criteria, or claim-to-paper map.
It does not present an MCORE-1 experiment, baseline comparison, or shard-recomposition result.
It spans multiple disciplines without demonstrating a direct bridge among them.
The original source file is retained outside this public repository.
limit.counterpoint: A broad conceptual synthesis can create useful hypotheses, but it becomes evidence only after a bounded, reproducible assay produces results.
not_established: Incremental value of MCORE metrics beyond Hamming distance, edit distance, hashes, or conventional integrity schemes.
Relevance to AI reasoning, AI safety, or neon physics.
A general scalability or distributed-systems advantage.

action.next_step: Create PSA-001 in the MCORE-1 research queue and execute the 128-trit, every-boundary, single-trit-flip first milestone.
action.release_gate: Publish a bounded claim only after reproducible zero-perturbation shard invariance, boundary manifests, prespecified baselines, and retained null results.

trace.receipt_manifest: External source: Neon_Perturbation_Shard_MetaAnalysis_Report(1).docx; June 2026; reviewed as a 6-page meta-analysis. Original intentionally not copied into this public repository.
source_locations: External source document: executive summary; Sections 3–5; references.
related_issues: PSA-001 to be created in the MCORE-1 research queue
